Bullet Point 5: Comes in a food safe standup resealable pouch Product Description: Start A Revolution In Your Mouth.
The War On Flavorless Food Is Over. HUZZAH

Back in the 18th century when ship voyages were long and often difficult, crews had to rely on limited foods to help them set sail. Shipboard food usually consisted of hardtack bread or ship’s biscuit and salted or dried beef. Some said the beef resembled "black oak" and sailors would sometimes carve this beef into buttons or belt buckles.

Boston Spice invites you to make a voyage into the land of homemade jerky. Hop aboard our “Black Oak Jerky” spice ship and make your own dried beef, venison, or duck jerky. Trust us, this spice will help you make your jerky something you will want to travel with.

Our blend has the bold flavors of cayenne, chipotle chile, coriander, and smoked paprika. Try Boston Spice’s “Black Oak Jerky” spice today. Raise the anchor, set the sails and hit the high seas with this specialty blend.

We are a spice blend company called BOSTON SPICE, located just outside of Boston Massachusetts. We created our line of revolutionary spice and seasoning blends because of our love of tasty flavorful food and the history behind the great city of Boston. We make spice blends and rubs, with a Boston twist. Every spice blend we make is handcrafted, hand blended, and hand packaged by us.

Here at BOSTON SPICE, we freshly prepare and blend natural dried herbs and spices to win the war over eating flavorless, flat, bland, dull, and one-dimensional foods.

Ingredients: kosher salt, sugar, garlic powder, chipotle chili, onion powder, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, coriander *all natural rice powder added to help reduce caking Value: 1.6 Unit: Ounce
overall_summary: Boston Spice positions its Black Oak Jerky seasoning as a premium, artisanal product with a bold flavor profile, emphasizing handcrafted quality and fresh blending. The resealable pouch and explicit focus on natural ingredients suggest a mid-to-high price point, targeting flavor enthusiasts and jerky makers. Absence of certifications or origin claims limits price justification to brand reputation and perceived craftsmanship.
